texts.full_text: f dollars The United Nations Child Sex Abuse Peacekeeping Scandal is an ongoing scandal that continues to plague the United Nations, even as recently as 2020. PBS News says there “have been more than 1,700 allegations of sexual abuse” by UN peacekeepers over the past 15 years. The exact number of countries the UN Sex Abuse scandal has plagued is not known, but Haiti, Cambodia, Mozambique, Bosnia, and Kosovo are among the victim nations. So widespread is the United Nations sex abuse scandal, a moniker has been coined for the children conceived in rape: “Peacekeeper Babies.” The Washington Post — not known for giving international aid programs a hard time — reports that the “U.N. system responsible for handling and prosecuting such cases has been widely criticized as dysfunctional.” International watchdog Human Rights Watch even goes as far as saying, “U.N. Peacekeeping has a sex abuse problem.” ABC referred to the revelations of abuse in 2015 as “the violent sexual abuse of children,” in which soldiers abused boys between the ages of eight and fifteen. Having been made aware of the scandal, the U.N. failed to take action to stop the abuse, and so a whistleblower leaked the internal report to the press. texts.full_text: can’t miss moment! And then we said goodbye. The kids cried. Heck, I CRIED! ***** On the way home, I got to thinkin’ “How could such an iconic brand fail? What are the lessons here for other businesses, because if Toys R Us can fall, any business can!” I realized, Toys R Us failed because they thought they were a toy store.They thought they just sold toys. But Toys R Us didn’t sell toys; they sold family time, the experience of taking your kids to the store. The light in your little girl’s eye when she saw the Barbie dolls. The raw excitement of your little boy holding his new nerf gun. Those experiences can’t be replicated by Wal-Mart.com or Amazon’s online marketplace. That was the Toys R Us advantage, but they didn’t realize it until it was too late. So what am I saying? We overcomplicate things in business. We get caught up in our products, our logos, our jingles. When really, all we have to do is focus on what truly matters. The customers’ experience. The joy of a problem solved. The desire to return and buy more stuff to feel that way again. That’s how to build a forever business. Goodby Toys R Us. Thanks for the memories and the business lessons on your way out. texts.full_text: PARTICIPATION IN 16 DAYS OF ACTIVISM AGAINST GENDER VIOLENCE Gabnet/Mariposa Alliance proudly affirm our participation in the international feminist movement's 16 Days of Activism against Gender Violence campaign. Along with over 2,000 organizations in 154 countries who have participated in this campaign since 1991, Gabnet/Ma-Al launches our own 16 days of activism, beginning on November 25th, the International Day Against Violence Against Women, with a nationwide Speak Out Against Violence, and ending on December 10th, International Human Rights Day.We in the U.S. are living in a time where words like progress, change and victory are flooding and infused in our collective discourse. We are supposed to believe that we have won the good fight; that there is nothing left to struggle for. texts.full_text: n modulation of cell shape and lipogenic gene expression in 3T3-adipocytes. The differentiation of 3T3-F442A cells is accompanied by changes in cell morphology, decreased synthesis and assembly of cytoskeletal proteins, and increased biosynthesis of lipogenic enzymes. The differentiation-dependent alterations in actin assembly can be prevented by culture of cells on fibronectin matrices. Under these conditions, morphological change is prevented and the induction of lipogenic enzymes such as glycerophosphate dehydrogenase and fatty acid synthetase is sharply reduced. In contrast, the differentiation-linked decreases in actin and tubulin biosynthesis occur normally. Hybridization of cytoplasmic and nuclear RNA to cloned cDNA probes indicates that the inhibition of lipogenic protein biosynthesis occurs because of reduced levels of specific mRNA production. texts.full_text: to meet their unique demands and enhance their ability to perform specific tasks. Take for example boiler engineers conducting an annual service, who could potentially use AR smart glasses to overlay the schematics of the boiler to enable a hands-free view of service procedures – meaning that when a fault becomes a barrier to repair, the engineer is able to use collaboration software to call for assistance from a remote expert. Elsewhere, in the healthcare sector smart eyewear may support clinicians with hands-free identification of patient records, medical procedures and information on medicines and results. Such examples demonstrate the immediate and diverse potential of wearables across different verticals. With enterprise IT infrastructure now in the position to embrace such technologies, it is this ability to deliver bespoke functionality to mobile workers which will be the catalyst for continued uptake throughout 2018 and beyond. texts.full_text: ts with T1D and repeated hypoglycaemic events. The aim of our study was to evaluate the frequency of hypoglycaemia unawareness and the continuous glucose profile in a group of subjects with Type 1 diabetes (T1D) with repeated non-severe/severe hypoglycaemia. Twenty patients (aged 35.2 +/- 7.6 years, duration of disease 16.4 +/- 6.4 years) were inclu...ded in the study. All participants had a history of recurrent non-severe and/or severe hypoglycaemic events, defined as at least one episode of severe hypoglycaemia (requiring assistance from another person) or more than two episodes of non-severe hypoglycaemia (blood glucose <70 mg/dL) in the past year. The study was conducted over a 3-month period, during which continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) data were collected using a Dexcom G6 CGM system. Hypoglycaemia unawareness was assessed using the Clarke score, with scores of 4 or higher indicating impaired awareness of hypoglycaemia. The glycaemic profile was evaluated based on CGM data, including time in range (TIR; 70-180 mg/dL), time below range (TBR; <70 mg/dL), and time above range (TAR; >180 mg/dL). Other metrics such as mean glucose levels, coefficient of variation (CV), and glycaemic variability were also calculated. Results showed that 14 out of 20 participants (70%) had impaired awareness of hypoglycaemia based on the Clarke score. The mean TIR was 58.3% ± 16.2%, with a mean TBR of 19.8% ± 14.1%. The mean glucose level was 172.4 mg/dL ± 30.1 mg/dL, and the CV was 35.8% ± 7.3%. These findings suggest that a significant proportion of individuals with T1D and recurrent hypoglycaemic events may have impaired awareness of hypoglycaemia. texts.full_text: student recruitment: "The 1994 Group is particularly concerned that there was a 14% fall in applications from students with AAB+ A-level grades. Regulation allows universities to recruit as many of these students as they like. "The fall in AAB+ students left many unfilled spaces at leading universities, which couldn’t be filled by other great students because of recruitment restrictions. "This issue, paired with concerns over international and postgraduate recruitment, is having a destabilising effect on our best universities. Restrictive student number controls prevent leading universities from having the flexibility to recruit great students. It is for that reason that we call on the Government to swiftly uncap recruitment on students who get BBB+ in their A-levels." Protect EU student applications for long-term economic success: "Finally, there has been a 20% fall in the number of applications from EU students to English universities. Deterring outstanding European students will inevitably have an impact on the intellectual and cultural diversity of our campuses and our economy. "We must not forget that the UK does half of its trade with Europe and so attracting EU students to our universities plays a key role in building those crucial long-term trading links upon which the UK relies." Scrobipalpa biskrae has a unique patterned appearance typical for its featuring dark-colored wings with various or streaks. The larvae are known as leaf miners; they burrow within the leaves of host plants, feeding on plant tissues without causing damage that would kill the plant. The habitat and range where Scrobipalpa biskrae is found can be influenced by several factors including climate conditions suitable for their development cycle. In North Africa's arid environment with its varying temperature patterns throughout seasons plays a crucial role in supporting this species' lifecycle, from egg to adult moth stages. as an insect pollinator or herbivore, Scrobipalpa biskrae contributes significantly within the ecosystem by participating in plant-pollination processes and serving as food for other organisms. Its life cycle also serves important ecological functions such as nutrient cycling through its feeding habits on plants which eventually decompose back into soil. Despite their small size compared to larger moth species, Scrobipalpa biskrae holds a significant role within the ecosystem dynamics of North Africa and plays an essential part in maintaining plant biodiversity. texts.full_text: GPU Implementation of adaptiveThreshold() in OpenCV Is there GPU implementation of adaptiveThresholdYes, OpenCV provides GPU-accelerated functions and algorithms, including adaptive thresholding, through its OpenCV-GPU module. This module, OpenCV-GPU, leverages the power of GPUs to significantly speed up certain image processing operations, including adaptive thresholding. To use the GPU implementation of adaptive thresholding, you would typically use the OpenCV-GPU module and its corresponding functions. However, the specific implementation and usage of the GPU adaptive thresholding function would depend on the programming language and OpenCV version you are using. For example, in Python, you would typically use the OpenCV-GPU module in conjunction with the OpenCV-Python bindings. The GPU-accelerated adaptive thresholding function would likely have a similar signature and usage to the CPU version, with the added benefit of GPU acceleration. Here's a basic example of how you might use the GPU adaptive thresholding function in OpenCV-Python, assuming the function is called "adaptiveThreshold_gpu": ```python import cv2 import cv2.gpu # Load the GPU-enabled OpenCV-Python bindings cv2.gpu.setSdkVersion(cv2.gpu.INITIAL_VERSION) # Load the image img_gpu = cv2.gpu.GpuMat() img_gpu.upload(img) # Apply GPU-accelerated adaptive thresholding threshold_gpu = cv2.gpu.adaptiveThreshold(img_gpu, 255, cv2.ADAPTIVE_THRESH_MEAN_C, cv2.THRESH_BINARY, 11, 2) # Download the thresholded image from the GPU threshold_img = threshold_gpu.download() # Display the thresholded image cv2.imshow("Adaptive Thresholded Image", threshold_img) cv2.waitKey(0) cv2.destroyAllWindows() ``` In this example, `img_gpu` is the input image, `cv2.gpu.adaptiveThreshold` is the GPU-accelerated adaptive thresholding function, and `cv2.gpu.GpuMat` is the GPU-accelerated image representation. texts.full_text: t as I delved deeper into the roots of my own beliefs and the structures of society, I found that the human need for meaning, purpose, and connection to something greater is not so easily discarded. In the absence of traditional religion, many radicals turn to ideologies or movements that offer a sense of belonging and direction. For some, it's environmentalism; for others, it's social justice, anarchism, or even the fight against industrial civilization itself. These causes can become the "gods" we serve, providing the moral framework and community that traditional religions once offered. The challenge lies in finding a balance where these commitments empower rather than enslave us. How do we live with conviction without falling into dogma? How do we stand for something without being consumed by it? How do we find purpose without losing our autonomy? For me, the answer has been to ground my radicalism in a deep connection to the Earth and a commitment to the survival of all its beings. This is not a return to traditional religion but a recognition that life itself is sacred and worthy of reverence. It's about acknowledging the interdependence of all living things and acting in ways that honor and protect that web of life. In this sense, my "god" is the living world, and my "masters" are the principles of justice, sustainability, and mutual aid. By aligning myself with these values, I find a sense of purpose that is both profound and liberating. It's a way of being in the world that doesn't require a supernatural deity but does demand a deep and abiding respect for the natural order. Living without gods or masters, then, is not about rejecting all forms of higher meaning. Instead, it's about finding a path that resonates with our deepest values and the realities of the world we inhabit. It's about creating a radical spirituality that is grounded in the earth and the struggles of those who fight for its preservation

Where quoted or used, they should be attributed clearly to the authors: Lynne Peterson, Tracy Burns and Mark Chalmers, ABS, Queensland Office. This study describes changes to the characteristics of the working population of 10 different regions within Queensland, as reported in each Census of Population and Housing from 1976 to 2001. Regional labour markets have differences in qualifications, income, age, hours worked and migration of workers from overseas and interstate, which are highlighted to understand the evolving structure of employment across Queensland’s geographic and economic landscape. These variations reflect shifts in industry composition, skill requirements, and demographic trends, providing insights into local workforce dynamics and informing policy decisions for sustainable economic development. This analysis underscores the importance of tailoring labour market strategies to regional needs, supported by longitudinal census data to track transitions in employment patterns over time. --- Explanation of Completion: 1. To prevent web2py from caching, you can take several approaches: 1. Development Mode: Ensure that your application is running in development mode, which typically disables many types of caching. In web2py, this is the default when you run the server using `web2py.py` without any specific options. 2. Disable App Caching: In the `models/db.py` or another model file, you can explicitly disable caching for your application by setting: ```python cache.ram.clear() cache.disk.clear() ``` This will clear the RAM and disk caches, which might help if your issue is related to these.

So yes, it makesense for you create a new project and start at version 1. This approach provides several benefits: Clear Versioning: Separating your internal API from public APIs allows clearer semantic versions in each space without the potential confusion of incrementally linking them together. For instance, if there's breaking change necessary with our website or an important feature we want to expose publicly while still developing and refining it within a controlled environment. Different User Needs: The internal API is typically used by other services (which could be part of your product but not directly user-facing), whereas the public APIs are exposed for developers. This means that each can evolve according specific needs, without interfering with or waiting on changes to another's ecosystem and version. Isolation During Development: By starting a new project at 1.x branch you have an opportunity during initial development (version numbers) of making misTakes in isolation which is always better than deploying directly into the main codebase. This allows for iterative improvement, feedback incorporation without affecting your core product or services that rely on stable internal APIs. Flexibility and Scalability: You can scale up with new versions independently based upon public API user's needs (and in some cases depending system integrators), while continuing to support older version of the Internal for existing applications/systems.
